title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-06
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window starts at `2026-05-05T12:23:46.853Z`. Signals show 0 issues, 0 pull requests, 2 discussions, and 0 commits updated.

Activity was limited to discussion updates: the prior daily summary received project-management comments, and a new self-improvement discussion explored proposal-first daily improvement workflows plus optional external inspiration scanning.

## Recently Active PRs

No pull requests were present in the synced signal files.

## Recently Active Issues

No issues were present in the synced signal files.

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#159](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/159) “Daily Summary — 2026-05-05”: received project-management summary comments; no managed priority/effort label changes were needed.
- [#160](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/160) “Ideas for self improvement”: discussed adding an opt-in daily self-improvement proposal workflow, with auto-orchestration deferred behind explicit config; also discussed optional related/trending repo inspiration as a later, source-linked lane.

## Follow-ups

- Convert discussion #160’s self-improvement workflow idea into a concrete tracking issue if the team wants to pursue it.
- Keep the first self-improvement version proposal-first and opt-in before considering automatic orchestration.
- No PR or issue follow-ups are visible from this synced activity window.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

## Project Management Summary

- Mode: `labels applied`
- Open items assessed: `39 issues, 13 pull requests`
- Managed labels: `priority/*`, `effort/*`

### Top Triage Queue

- `pull_request#158` Add approval-gated agent failure diagnosis — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Active replacement for the older failure-reporting UX; central to safer bug diagnosis/reporting before public release. Label changes: none.
- `issue#156` Rethink bug resolution, automatic diagnosis, and automatic reporting — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Current accepted design source for failure diagnosis and approval-gated reporting, with PR #158 in progress. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#149` Add deterministic setup apply route — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Important privileged setup path in the #126 project-management stack; review/merge unblocks later setup automation. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#147` Add plan-only setup route — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Foundation for setup planning UX and stacked setup work. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#143` Add Project target config for project management — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Threads project-backed planning configuration into the project-manager flow; key to moving beyond label-first planning. Label changes: none.
- `issue#126` What's the best way to manage the tasks and project planning for this repo now? — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Parent planning track for Project-backed task management and staged setup work. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#133` Add Sepo version metadata reader CLI — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Supports update, installation, and failure-report identity work. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#120` Add GitHub App installation bootstrap — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — High-impact onboarding/distribution automation. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#116` Add scheduled Sepo update checks — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — User-side update path for installed Sepo workflows; important distribution milestone. Label changes: none.
- `issue#75` Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Concrete workflow reliability bug that can drop valid agent invocations. Label changes: none.

### Label Changes

Two stale managed-label updates are planned for deterministic application: the original auto-reporting issue/PR have been superseded by the safer #156/#158 approval-gated design and should no longer occupy high or medium-effort triage slots.

```json
{
  "label_changes": [
    {
      "kind": "issue",
      "number": 26,
      "add": ["priority/p3", "effort/low"],
      "remove": ["priority/p2", "effort/high"]
    },
    {
      "kind": "pull_request",
      "number": 117,
      "add": ["effort/low"],
      "remove": ["effort/medium"]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Notes

- Open items are dominated by two active workstreams: Project-backed setup/planning under #126 and public-release distribution/reliability work.
- Several child issues under #126 are marked done in their bodies but remain open while their stacked draft PRs are still open.
- No labels were mutated directly by the agent; changes above are limited to the managed-label plan for the deterministic workflow step.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 6 managed priority/effort label operation(s).
